request.system: You are a regulatory analyst. Write a 2-3 sentence storyline summary (≤500 chars total) of the regulatory pressure history for this prediction-market contract. For 'retrospective' kind, write past tense and reference the actual resolution. For 'active' kind, write present tense and note what's still pending. Return JSON {text: ...}.
request.user:
Contract: Marijuana rescheduled by December 31, 2026?
Kind: active
Timeline (5 events):
- 2026-05-13 [background] Attorney General Bonta: Joint Investigation Leads to Felony Charges Against Unlicensed Cannabis Retailer for $7.1 Million in Tax Evasion - 13.05.2026 — State tax prosecution of an unlicensed cannabis retailer is a local enforcement action and has no clear effect on the DEA's federal rescheduling decision.
- 2026-05-13 [background] Attorney General Bonta: Joint Investigation Leads to Felony Charges Against Unlicensed Cannabis Retailer for $7.1 Million in Tax Evasion — State tax/evasion charges against an unlicensed CA cannabis retailer are local enforcement and do not materially affect a federal DEA rescheduling decision.
- 2026-05-11 [background] AG Rayfield, State AGs Warn of Enforcement Gap as Feds Pull Back on Corporate Oversight — State AGs warning about federal pullback on corporate oversight doesn't materially affect DEA's decision on rescheduling marijuana; no direct signal.
- 2026-03-26 [background] Attorney General Alan Wilson announces indictments in State Grand Jury Operation ‘Ganjapreneur’ involving marijuana and illegal THC products being sold openly in SC businesses — South Carolina Attorney General — State Grand Jury Division — South Carolina Securities Commissioner: tangential to resolution, no directional effect.
- 2025-12-30 [background] AG Campbell Secures Agreement With Trump Administration Preventing Further Delays In Medical And Public Health Research — Office of the Attorney General — Massachusetts Office of the Attorney General Consumer Advocacy & Response Division: tangential to resolution, no directional effect.

response.text: So far, the timeline features state AG crackdowns on unlicensed cannabis sales and general oversight warnings—local actions with no clear bearing on DEA’s federal rescheduling. No direct federal signal appears; the decisive step remains DEA rulemaking on marijuana’s schedule, with any proposed or final rule and interagency review still pending ahead of the 2026 year-end deadline.
